questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The bar chart shows four values: $10, 20, 30, 40$. Compute their total.

figure.caption: Bar chart of four values
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='100' width='25' height='20'/><rect x='85' y='80' width='25' height='40'/><rect x='125' y='60' width='25' height='60'/><rect x='165' y='40' width='25' height='80'/></g><text x='50' y='138' font-size='8'>10, 20, 30, 40</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add the values.

**Working.**
$$10+20+30+40=100$$

**Answer.** $100$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The square root of $196$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Find the number whose square is $196$.

**Working.** $14^2 = 196$.

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the best connective: '____ the equipment was old, it functioned reliably.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A contrast between two clauses is introduced by a concessive conjunction.

**Working.** Old equipment working reliably is unexpected, so a contrast word ('Although') is needed; 'Because' would imply causation.

**Answer.** Although.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the perimeter of the rectangle shown with length $8$ and width $3$.

figure.caption: Rectangle
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='40' width='120' height='50' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><text x='95' y='105' font-size='8'>l=8</text><text x='12' y='68' font-size='8'>w=3</text></svg>

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the option that correctly completes the sentence: 'By the time the rescue team arrived, the trapped miners ____ for nearly ten hours.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An action continuing up to a point in the past uses the past perfect continuous tense.

**Working.** The reference point ('the team arrived') is in the past, and the waiting was ongoing before it. This requires 'had been + verb-ing'.

**Answer.** had been waiting.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A is twice as efficient as B, and together they finish a job in $12$ days. A alone would take
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Let B's rate be $x$; then A's rate is $2x$.

**Working.** $3x=\dfrac{1}{12}\Rightarrow x=\dfrac{1}{36}$; A's rate $=2x=\dfrac{1}{18}$, so A alone takes $18$ days.

**Answer.** $18$ days.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: How many triangles are there in the square with both diagonals drawn (shown)?

figure.caption: A square with both diagonals drawn, forming four triangular regions.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 130 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='15' y='15' width='100' height='100' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='15' y1='15' x2='115' y2='115' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='115' y1='15' x2='15' y2='115' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count the small triangles and the larger ones formed by pairs.

**Working.** There are $4$ small triangles plus $4$ larger triangles (each half of the square), giving $8$.

**Answer.** $8$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Which word reads the same when viewed in a vertical mirror?

figure.caption: The word MOM with a dashed vertical mirror line to its right.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><text x='30' y='72' font-size='52' fill='#334155'>MOM</text><line x1='195' y1='12' x2='195' y2='98' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each letter must be vertically symmetric and the word a palindrome.

**Working.** M, O, M are all vertically symmetric and 'MOM' is a palindrome, so the mirror image is identical.

**Answer.** MOM.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one interior angle is $70^\circ$ (see figure), its co-interior (same-side) angle is

figure.caption: Two parallel lines crossed by a transversal; one interior angle is marked 70 degrees.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='60' x2='200' y2='60' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='20' y1='120' x2='200' y2='120' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='60' y1='30' x2='160' y2='150' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><text x='95' y='78' font-size='11' fill='#ef4444'>70</text><text x='118' y='112' font-size='11' fill='#10b981'>?</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Co-interior (same-side interior) angles between parallel lines are supplementary.

**Working.** $180^\circ-70^\circ=110^\circ$.

**Answer.** $110^\circ$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next term: $3, 7, 15, 31, \,?$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each term follows $a_{n+1}=2a_n+1$.

**Working.** $31\times 2 + 1 = 63$.

**Answer.** $63$.

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: For the settling tank shown, read the flow $Q$ (MLD) and plan area $A$ from the diagram. The surface overflow rate $Q/A$ is _____ m³/m²/day.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="40" width="250" height="90" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M20 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="14" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">Q = 6 MLD</text><path d="M290 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="300" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">out</text><line x1="40" y1="48" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="120" cy="80" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="170" cy="100" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="210" cy="118" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><path d="M120 86 L132 124 M170 106 L182 126" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="0.8"/><text x="120" y="150" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">plan area A = 400 m²</text><text x="160" y="36" font-size="8" fill="#2563eb">surface (overflow)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Flow Q and plan area A marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** SOR = Q/A, with Q and A read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{6\times1000}{400}=15.$$

**Answer.** $15$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: A landfill has a usable air-space of $5,00,000\,\text{m}^3$ and receives compacted waste at $50,000\,\text{m}^3/\text{yr}$. Its design life is _____ years.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><rect x="30" y="40" width="280" height="86" fill="#a16207" opacity="0.5"/><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="10" fill="#65a30d"/><rect x="30" y="126" width="280" height="14" fill="#1e3a8a" opacity="0.6"/><rect x="30" y="140" width="280" height="10" fill="#0f172a"/><text x="170" y="26" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">final cover / vegetation</text><text x="170" y="86" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">compacted MSW + daily cover</text><text x="170" y="137" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">leachate collection</text><text x="170" y="148" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">composite liner (clay+HDPE)</text><path d="M315 60 q14 0 14 14 q0 10 -8 18" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="318" y="56" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">gas vent</text></svg>
figure.caption: Engineered sanitary landfill: cover, waste cell, liner and leachate system.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life $=\dfrac{\text{capacity}}{\text{annual fill}}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{500000}{50000}=10.$$

**Answer.** $10\,\text{yr}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: An uncompensated environmental cost imposed on a third party by an economic activity is called a(n)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Pollution damage to others is a negative externality the polluter-pays principle internalises.

**Working.**
Negative externality.

**Answer.** Negative externality

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays by first-order kinetics with rate constant $k=0.1\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Its half-life is _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For first order, $t_{1/2}=\dfrac{\ln 2}{k}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.693}{0.1}=6.93.$$

**Answer.** $6.93\,\text{d}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $5$ identical sources each $75\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$75+10\log5=81.99.$$

**Answer.** $81.99\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: In a reactor, $30\,\text{mg/L}$ of biomass is produced while $120\,\text{mg/L}$ of substrate is consumed. The observed yield $Y_{X/S}$ is _____ mg/mg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Y_{X/S}=\dfrac{\Delta X}{\Delta S}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{30}{120}=0.25.$$

**Answer.** $0.25$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 300 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 1.5 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle settles the marked depth in the marked time; v_s = depth ÷ time. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** v_s = depth ÷ time (read both from the trajectory).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{1.5\,\text{m}}{300\,\text{s}}\times1000=5\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $5\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Read the loose and compacted densities from the bar chart. The volume reduction $(1-\rho_1/\rho_2)$ is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="132.9" width="50" height="17.1" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="128.9" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">100</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">loose ρ₁</text><rect x="180" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">700</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">compacted ρ₂</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kg/m³</text></svg>
figure.caption: Loose and compacted densities shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{100}{700}\right)\times100=85.7\%.$$

**Answer.** $85.7\%$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: In the energy pyramid shown, only the producer (trophic-1) energy is marked; 10% transfers per level. Energy at trophic level $3$ is _____ kcal/m²/yr.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 180" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><polygon points="40,150 280,150 240,118 80,118" fill="#16a34a" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="80,118 240,118 205,86 115,86" fill="#65a30d" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="115,86 205,86 178,54 142,54" fill="#d97706" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="142,54 178,54 165,30 155,30" fill="#dc2626" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="160" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">Producers = 15000</text><text x="160" y="110" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L2 = ?</text><text x="160" y="78" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L3 = ?</text><text x="160" y="46" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L4 = ?</text><text x="160" y="172" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kcal/m²/yr — 10% transfer per level</text></svg>
figure.caption: Only the producer (trophic level 1) energy is marked; upper levels follow the 10% rule. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Eₙ = E₀(0.1)ⁿ, with E₀ read from the pyramid.

**Working.**
$$15000\times0.1^{2}=150.$$

**Answer.** $150$

$$10000\times0.1^{3}=10.$$

**Answer.** $10\,\text{kcal/m}^2/\text{yr}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $1500\,\text{kg}$ at $60\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$1500(1-60/100)=600.$$

**Answer.** $600\,\text{kg}$

questions.stem: For the rectangular settling tank shown, read the flow $Q$ (in MLD) and the plan area $A$ from the diagram. The surface overflow rate $Q/A$ is _____ m³/m²/day.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="40" width="250" height="90" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M20 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="14" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">Q = 8 MLD</text><path d="M290 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="300" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">out</text><line x1="40" y1="48" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="120" cy="80" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="170" cy="100" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="210" cy="118" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><path d="M120 86 L132 124 M170 106 L182 126" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="0.8"/><text x="120" y="150" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">plan area A = 400 m²</text><text x="160" y="36" font-size="8" fill="#2563eb">surface (overflow)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Flow Q and plan area A marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $SOR=\dfrac{Q}{A}$ (1 MLD = 1000 m³/day), with $Q$ and $A$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{8\times1000}{400}=20.$$

**Answer.** $20\,\text{m/day}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: An exporter's overseas buyers demand third-party certification of an audited, continually-improving system that reduces the environmental impact of its operations — not product quality, not worker safety, not information security. The exporter should certify to
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Match the scope to the standard: ISO 14001 = Environmental Management System (PDCA over environmental aspects); ISO 9001 = quality; ISO 45001 = occupational health & safety; ISO 27001 = information security.

**Working.**
Scope is environmental impact ⇒ ISO 14001.

**Answer.** ISO 14001

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: Which processes are strictly anaerobic in wastewater treatment?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Methanogenesis requires strict anaerobiosis. Denitrification is anoxic (uses NO₃⁻), nitrification and carbon oxidation are aerobic.

**Working.**
Only methanogenesis is strictly anaerobic.

**Answer.** Methanogenesis

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: A treatment plant receives BOD $200\,\text{mg/L}$ and discharges $30\,\text{mg/L}$. The BOD removal efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 200</text><rect x="190" y="132" width="46" height="18" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="128" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 30</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">BOD (mg/L)</text></svg>
figure.caption: BOD: influent vs. effluent (mg/L).

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\eta=\dfrac{S_0-S_e}{S_0}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{200-30}{200}\times100=85\%.$$

**Answer.** $85\%$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: At a receptor, $10$ identical machines each produce $75\,\text{dB}$. The combined sound pressure level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $L=L_1+10\log_{10}n$ for $n$ equal incoherent sources.

**Working.**
$$75+10\log_{10}10=85.$$

**Answer.** $85\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $6\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$. Reading the collecting area $A$ and drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 200 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.12 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** η = 1 − e^{−wA/Q}, with A and w read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$(1-e^{-0.12\times200/6})\times100=98.17\%.$$

**Answer.** $98.17\%$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics. Reading $\mu_{max}$ (plateau) and the half-saturation constant $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate at substrate $S=100\,\text{mg/L}$ is _____ h⁻¹.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Monod: $\mu=\dfrac{\mu_{max}S}{K_s+S}$, with $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ read from the curve.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 200" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><line x1="34" y1="30" x2="34" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="34" y1="175" x2="330" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="8" y="40" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">DO</text><text x="305" y="190" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">distance/time →</text><line x1="34" y1="45" x2="330" y2="45" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="250" y="42" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">saturation DO</text><path d="M34 60 Q120 175 175 150 Q260 110 330 70" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="175" y1="45" x2="175" y2="150" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="175" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="180" y="120" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">D_c = 2.5 mg/L</text><text x="150" y="170" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">t_c = 2.5 d</text></svg>
figure.caption: Critical deficit D_c (and t_c) marked on the sag curve only. (read values from the figure)

**Concept.** Minimum DO = DO_sat − D_c, with D_c read from the sag curve.

**Concept.** Beyond the breakpoint, extra chlorine appears as free residual: applied = breakpoint dose + desired free residual.

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=40\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.3</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 50</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.3\times40}{50+40}=0.133.$$

**Answer.** $0.133$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $8\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$. Reading the collecting area $A$ and drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 200 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.12 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** η = 1 − e^{−wA/Q}, with A and w read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$(1-e^{-0.12\times200/8})\times100=95.02\%.$$

**Answer.** $95.02\%$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 400 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 1.5 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle settles the marked depth in the marked time; v_s = depth ÷ time. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** v_s = depth ÷ time (read both from the trajectory).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{1.5\,\text{m}}{400\,\text{s}}\times1000=3.75\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $3.75\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: The stable, self-perpetuating community reached at the end of ecological succession is the
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Succession culminates in a climax community in equilibrium with the environment.

**Working.**
Climax community.

**Answer.** Climax community

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Modified Dulong $HHV=337C+1419(H-O/8)$ kJ/kg with $C=45\%$, $H=5\%$, $O=40\%$. HHV is _____ kJ/kg.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 15.2</text><rect x="190" y="146" width="46" height="4" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 0</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">HHV (MJ/kg)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Energy content of the waste fuel.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the modified Dulong formula.

**Working.**
$$337(45)+1419(5-40/8)=15165.$$

**Answer.** $15165\,\text{kJ/kg}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Which characteristics define a hazardous waste under typical regulatory frameworks?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The four hazard characteristics are ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity and toxicity; moisture content is not one.

**Working.**
Ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity.

**Answer.** Ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays first-order with $k=0.3\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Reading the initial concentration $C_0$ from the figure, its concentration after $3$ d is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 170" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="120" y="100" width="60" height="50" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.25" stroke="#1e293b"/><rect x="120" y="30" width="60" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t = 0</text><text x="150" y="96" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">C₀ = 100</text><text x="150" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = C₀e^{−kt}, with C₀ read from the figure.

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Ammonia-bearing water is chlorinated. From the breakpoint-chlorination curve shown, read the breakpoint dose. To leave a free residual of $1.5\,\text{mg/L}$, the chlorine that must be applied is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="20" x2="38" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="4" y="30" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">residual</text><text x="232" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">Cl₂ dose (mg/L) →</text><path d="M38 165 L95 120 L150 150 L205 160 L300 80" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="205" y1="55" x2="205" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="205" cy="160" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="170" y="50" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">breakpoint</text><text x="186" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">7</text><text x="250" y="92" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">free residual</text></svg>
figure.caption: Breakpoint dose marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Beyond the breakpoint, extra chlorine appears as free residual: applied = breakpoint dose + desired free residual.

**Working.**
$$7+1.5=8.5.$$

**Answer.** $8.5\,\text{mg/L}$

questions.solution:

**Working.**
$$80+10\log10=90.$$

**Answer.** $90\,\text{dB}$

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 188" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="36" y1="150" x2="304" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="60" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="83" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">50</text><text x="83" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂</text><rect x="142" y="140.4" width="46" height="9.6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="165" y="136.4" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">4</text><text x="165" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CH₄</text><rect x="224" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="247" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">1</text><text x="247" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">N₂O</text><text x="170" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Σ massᵢ × GWPᵢ, with the three masses read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$50+4\times28+1\times265=427.$$

**Answer.** $427$

**Working.**
$$9-3=6.$$

**Answer.** $6\,\text{mg/L}$
